description: §14.4/§18.7 — a TRAILING comma in a variant construction is valid scrml (`[1,2,3,]`, `h("a",)`) and MUST NOT over-count as an extra argument. `fail MyError.NotFound("x",)` (fail locus, raw-string arg count) and `let e = MyError.NotFound("y",)` (non-fail locus, acorn drops the trailing comma) each bind the sole `id` field — 1 arg / 1 field. Regression guard: the fail-locus raw-string counter must match acorn's trailing-comma-dropping behavior so it does not false-fire E-TYPE-082 on well-formed trailing-comma construction. `notCodes` asserts E-TYPE-082 does not fire.
rationale: §14.4: construction supplies one positional argument per declared field; a trailing comma is syntactic sugar, not an argument, and SHALL NOT fire E-TYPE-082.
